name: Roast Pork Loin with Sage Crust and Apple Brandy Sauce
recipeInstructions:
1. In a shallow dish, combine chopped sage, garlic, olive oil, and crushed peppercorns.
Place the pork loin in the dish and coat all sides with the sage-garlic mixture.
Refrigerate for at least 8 hours or overnight.
2. Preheat the oven to 450 degrees F (230 degrees C).
Place the pork on a rack in a roasting pan and roast for 15 minutes.
Lower the temperature to 350 degrees F and continue cooking until the internal temperature reaches 160 degrees F, about 25-30 more minutes.
3. Transfer the meat to a hot platter and cover with foil or place it in a warm oven to keep warm.
Let rest for several minutes before slicing.
4. In a saucepan, heat olive oil over medium heat.
Add shallots and sauté until lightly browned, about 3-5 minutes.
Add apples and basil, stirring to combine.
Pour in Calvados brandy and ignite with a long match (or pour into a heat-safe ladle and ignite).
Stir in the pan drippings and remaining veal stock; reduce sauce over low heat for 15 minutes.
5. Dissolve cornstarch in cold water, then stir it into the sauce.
Bring to a boil and cook until thickened, about 1 minute.
Season with salt and pepper before serving alongside the pork.
